mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad demand contraction that suppresses corporate revenue growth, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory shortages that trigger short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following INDPRO declines during post-shock recovery periods, with t-statistic < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Tight monetary policy combined with a cooling labor market creates a stagflationary environment that severely penalizes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized indicates severe economic deceleration that forces earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and consumer spending capacity, generating positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS increases, with Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy forces central banks to maintain elevated rates longer, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, with mean return > 0.5% and t-statistic > 2.0.
